mcp1_academix.note: All 15 returned papers are generic LLM/AI-in-education papers (ChatGPT healthcare, LLaMA, SWOT analysis, etc.). None are specifically about L2 writing feedback accuracy at the category level.

mcp2_papersearch.source: unified (arxiv, pubmed, biorxiv, medrxiv, semantic, crossref, openalex, pmc, core, europepmc, openaire)

new_papers.title: AI-Driven Intelligent Feedback System for Enhancing Self-Assessment Accuracy in Higher Education Writing
new_papers.abstract: Uses BERT/RoBERTa to support personalised writing evaluation. Quasi-experimental design found no significant group-level difference in posttest SAA, but interaction analysis revealed compensatory effect: students with lower baseline accuracy benefited substantially from LLMF, while higher-baseline students showed limited change.
new_papers.proposed_section: §1.3 Accuracy and Quality of LLM-Generated Feedback OR §1.2 Student Response
new_papers.finding: LLM feedback has a compensatory effect — helps low-accuracy students more than high-accuracy ones. Directly relevant to the r=.816 sobering implication from Bai & Hu (2017).

new_papers.title: Continuation Wing 1.0 - A Deepseek-Assisted Feedback Platform for Continuation Writing Assessment
new_papers.authors: Zheng Li; Bao Chen; Ziying Ling; Rixi Wu; Da Ling
new_papers.abstract: DeepSeek-assisted continuation writing platform with interpretable scoring (semantic matching, grammatical error density, discourse coherence). QWK 0.81 (Content), 0.79 (Language). Eight-week intervention showed clear gains in content, language accuracy, and coherence.
new_papers.finding: Classroom-speed DeepSeek platform for continuation writing; demonstrates that interpretable scoring + targeted feedback improves L2 writing outcomes. Supports multi-LLM design logic.

new_papers.title: Raising the linguistic accuracy level of advanced L2 writers with written corrective feedback
new_papers.abstract: No abstract in source, but title indicates study on written corrective feedback effects on advanced L2 writers' linguistic accuracy.
new_papers.finding: Key AWE corrective feedback study. Directly relevant to the category-level accuracy lineage (Dikli & Bleyle 2014 → Lavolette et al. 2015 → Bai & Hu 2017) that the proposal builds on.

new_papers.title: LLM feedback for academic writing: Effects on students' performance and engagement
new_papers.authors: Robert Glüsing; Johanna Fleckenstein; Fabian Schmidt; Jens Möller
new_papers.abstract: No abstract in source (SSRN preprint).
new_papers.finding: Directly examines LLM feedback effects on academic writing performance and engagement. Fleckenstein co-author — connects to the meta-analysis already cited.

new_papers.title: Emotional Engagement Prediction and LLM Feedback in Smart Learning Environments
new_papers.authors: Miada Almasre; Wafaa Alsaggaf; Norah Al-Malki; Hani Brdesee
new_papers.abstract: No abstract available in source.
new_papers.finding: Links emotional engagement with LLM feedback — relevant to Liu & Liang (2026) finding that AI's strongest effect is emotional (d=0.628).

new_papers.title: Navigating AI feedback in translation training: how text type, proficiency, and attitude shape students' acceptance behaviors
new_papers.abstract: 78 translation students, 68.2% acceptance rate of LLM suggestions. Acceptance shaped by text type (technical/news highest, literary/tourism lowest). Higher-proficiency students more critical. Rejection triggered deeper engagement — self-revision, external verification, AI dialogue.
new_papers.finding: 68.2% acceptance rate for AI feedback; rejection triggers deeper cognitive processing than acceptance. Directly relevant to the six-category uptake taxonomy proposal.

new_papers.title: LLMs as Writing Assistants: Exploring Perspectives on Sense of Ownership and Reasoning
new_papers.authors: Azmine Toushik Wasi; Mst Rafia Islam; Raima Islam
new_papers.abstract: Survey examining sense of ownership in writing with LLM assistants. Found users credit LLMs more in creative tasks, and may not claim ownership of LLM-generated content but freely claim authorship.
new_papers.finding: Addresses authorship/ownership perceptions — related to voice homogenization concern (Yang & Li, 2024; Helm & Hesse, 2026) but from student perspective.

new_papers.title: Voice Interaction With Conversational AI Could Facilitate Thoughtful Reflection and Substantive Revision in Writing
new_papers.authors: Jiho Kim; Philippe Laban; Xiang 'Anthony' Chen; Kenneth C. Arnold
new_papers.abstract: Proposes voice-based interaction with LLM-powered conversational agents for writing feedback. Argues voice can encourage engagement with higher-order concerns, iterative reflection, and reduce cognitive load vs text.
new_papers.finding: Voice interaction as alternative feedback modality — potentially relevant for remote data collection design.

new_papers.title: Individual differences and written corrective feedback: exploring the effects of direct and coded feedback on Chinese EFL learners' writing accuracy
new_papers.abstract: HKU PhD dissertation on individual differences in WCF effects.
new_papers.finding: Individual differences moderate WCF effectiveness — relevant to Study 3 moderation analysis (proficiency, AIFL, self-efficacy).

query2.query: peer teacher feedback comparison accuracy second language writing

mcp1_academix.note: Query in file is 'AI feedback uptake revision quality meta-analysis' but returned mostly medical/irrelevant papers. One relevant paper found: Banihashem et al. (2024).

new_papers.title: Feedback sources in essay writing: peer-generated or AI-generated feedback?
new_papers.authors: Seyyed Kazem Banihashem; Nafiseh Taghizadeh Kerman; Omid Noroozi; Jewoong Moon; Hendrik Drachsler
new_papers.journal: International Journal of Educational Technology in Higher Education
new_papers.abstract: Compared ChatGPT-generated feedback with peer feedback for argumentative essay writing among 74 Dutch graduate students. ChatGPT provided more descriptive feedback; peers provided feedback including information. Significant difference between the two sources.
new_papers.finding: Direct comparison of peer vs AI feedback quality in essay writing. Supports the three-way comparison design in Study 1.

new_papers.title: Impact of ChatGPT on ESL students' academic writing skills: a mixed methods intervention study
new_papers.abstract: Found significant positive impact of ChatGPT on ESL students' academic writing skills. Students' perceptions overwhelmingly positive.
new_papers.finding: ALREADY IN PROPOSAL reference list (Mahapatra, 2024). Included here for completeness.

mcp2_papersearch.source: unified (arxiv, pubmed, biorxiv, medrxiv, semantic, crossref, openalex, pmc, core, europepmc, openaire)

new_papers.title: Exposía: Teaching and Assessment of Academic Writing Skills for Research Project Proposals and Peer Feedback
new_papers.authors: Dennis Zyska; Alla Rozovskaya; Ilia Kuznetsov; Iryna Gurevych
new_papers.abstract: First public dataset connecting writing and feedback in higher education. Includes student research proposals and peer/instructor feedback. Benchmarked LLMs on automated scoring of proposals and reviews. Closed-source models outperform open-weight models.
new_papers.proposed_section: §1.4 Student Response and Uptake OR §3.3 Study 1
new_papers.finding: New dataset connecting writing and feedback. LLM benchmarking for scoring — relevant to Study 2 classifier design.

new_papers.title: Effects of teacher-scaffolded and self-scaffolded corrective feedback compared to direct corrective feedback on grammatical accuracy in English L2 writing
new_papers.abstract: No abstract in source.
new_papers.finding: Compares teacher-scaffolded, self-scaffolded, and direct corrective feedback on L2 grammatical accuracy. Directly relevant to the feedback-type comparison in Study 1.

new_papers.title: The effect of peer and teacher feedback on student writing
new_papers.abstract: No abstract in source.
new_papers.finding: Classic peer vs teacher feedback study in JSLW. Foundational reference for the three-way comparison.

new_papers.title: Mediating L2 writing revision: The impact of teacher-moderated ChatGPT feedback on L2 accuracy and lexical diversity
new_papers.abstract: No abstract in source.
new_papers.finding: Teacher-moderated ChatGPT feedback effects on L2 accuracy and lexical diversity. Directly supports the hybrid feedback condition in Study 1.

new_papers.title: Using teacher-supported peer feedback to develop student feedback literacy in L2 writing: An instructional model
new_papers.abstract: Proposes an instructional model of teacher-supported peer feedback for developing student feedback literacy from a self-regulatory perspective.
new_papers.finding: Teacher-supported peer feedback model for developing feedback literacy. Links to Carless & Boud (2018) framework already in proposal.

new_papers.title: A comparative study of peer and teacher feedback in a Chinese EFL writing class
new_papers.authors: Miao Yang; Richard Badger; Zhen Yu
new_papers.abstract: No abstract in source.
new_papers.finding: Highly cited (366) peer vs teacher feedback comparison in Chinese EFL context. Directly relevant to the three-way comparison design.

new_papers.title: Peer feedback and Chinese medical students' English academic writing development: a longitudinal intervention study
new_papers.authors: Chenze Wu; Yao-Wu Zhang; Albert W. Li
new_papers.abstract: Longitudinal intervention showing peer feedback significantly improved Chinese medical students' English academic writing (overall score, task response, coherence, lexical resource, grammar). Effects retained at 6-week delayed post-test.
new_papers.finding: Peer feedback effectiveness for L2 medical academic writing with delayed post-test durability — relevant to Study 3 delayed post-test design.

new_papers.title: Beyond the monorail perspective: constructing a dual-role engagement framework in peer feedback for second language oral communication
new_papers.abstract: Proposes dual-role engagement framework for peer feedback: learners as both feedback providers and recipients. Supported by sociocultural theory and cooperative learning theory.
new_papers.finding: Dual-role framework for peer feedback engagement. Complements Lin & Yu (2025) dual-role framework already cited in proposal.

new_papers.title: The Impact of Positive Peer Feedback on Second Language Writing Accuracy
new_papers.abstract: Compared direct focused peer feedback vs direct focused teacher feedback on L2 writing accuracy (tense uptake) with 58 Turkish EFL students. Both feedback types had significant effects on writing accuracy.
new_papers.finding: Peer vs teacher feedback comparison for L2 writing accuracy. Supports the three-way comparison rationale.

new_papers.title: Using Large Language Models to Provide Explanatory Feedback to Human Tutors
new_papers.authors: Jionghao Lin; Danielle R. Thomas; Feifei Han; Shivang Gupta; Wei Tan; Ngoc Dang Nguyen; Kenneth R. Koedinger
new_papers.abstract: Uses LLM-facilitated named entity recognition to provide real-time explanatory feedback to tutors during online lessons on how to give effective praise.
new_papers.finding: LLM feedback for tutor training — not L2 writing directly but shows LLM-as-explanatory-feedback approach.

Developing a comprehensive uptake taxono.note: Not found in any of the 4 MCP files. Proposal cites Zhang & Hyland (2018) and Zhang (2020) — different papers. If this paper exists, it was not retrieved by either search query.

AI-Driven Intelligent Feedback System fo.note: FOUND. Published in Expert Systems (doi: 10.1111/exsy.70184). Uses BERT/RoBERTa for self-assessment accuracy. Compensatory effect for low-accuracy students.

peer vs teacher feedback comparison pape.note: None of these specific papers were retrieved by either search query. However, other peer-vs-teacher papers WERE found: Paulus (1999), Yang, Badger & Yu (2006), Banihashem et al. (2024).

When and How Does LLM-Generated Feedback.note: Already in proposal as Zhang, D.-W., Hong, X., & Qi, Y. (2025). Two copies found (single-author preprint and three-author final).

AWE feedback papers about category-level.note: Not found in any MCP file. However, Bitchener & Knoch (2010) WAS found — a closely related WCF accuracy study.

Prober.ai (Bi, Wei & Zhou 2026).note: Already in proposal as Bi, Wei & Zhou (2026).

papers_to_add.title: AI-Driven Intelligent Feedback System for Enhancing Self-Assessment Accuracy in Higher Education Writing
papers_to_add.section: §1.3 Accuracy and Quality of LLM-Generated Feedback
papers_to_add.finding: Compensatory effect: LLM feedback benefits low-accuracy students more than high-accuracy ones. Supports the Bai & Hu (2017) r=.816 concern that the students who most need help are least able to evaluate it.

papers_to_add.title: Feedback sources in essay writing: peer-generated or AI-generated feedback?
papers_to_add.finding: Direct comparison of ChatGPT vs peer feedback quality for argumentative essays — ChatGPT provided more descriptive feedback. Supports the three-way feedback comparison design.

papers_to_add.title: A comparative study of peer and teacher feedback in a Chinese EFL writing class
papers_to_add.finding: Classic highly-cited (366) peer vs teacher feedback comparison in Chinese EFL context. Direct precedent for the three-way comparison in Study 1.

papers_to_add.title: The effect of peer and teacher feedback on student writing
papers_to_add.finding: Foundational (251 citations) peer vs teacher feedback study in JSLW. Establishes the research lineage for the three-way comparison.

papers_to_add.title: Mediating L2 writing revision: The impact of teacher-moderated ChatGPT feedback on L2 accuracy and lexical diversity
papers_to_add.finding: Teacher-moderated ChatGPT feedback improves L2 accuracy and lexical diversity. Directly supports the hybrid feedback condition and the four-category teacher modification taxonomy.

papers_to_add.title: Using teacher-supported peer feedback to develop student feedback literacy in L2 writing: An instructional model
papers_to_add.finding: Teacher-supported peer feedback model for developing feedback literacy from a self-regulatory perspective. Links peer feedback to the feedback literacy framework already in proposal.

papers_to_add.title: Effects of teacher-scaffolded and self-scaffolded corrective feedback compared to direct corrective feedback on grammatical accuracy in English L2 writing
papers_to_add.finding: Comparison of scaffolded vs direct corrective feedback on L2 grammatical accuracy. Relevant to feedback mode dimension in Study 1 coding scheme.

papers_to_add.title: Exposía: Teaching and Assessment of Academic Writing Skills for Research Project Proposals and Peer Feedback
papers_to_add.finding: First public dataset connecting writing and peer/instructor feedback in higher education. Benchmarked LLMs on scoring — relevant to Study 2 classifier design.

papers_to_add.title: Raising the linguistic accuracy level of advanced L2 writers with written corrective feedback
papers_to_add.section: §1.3 Accuracy and Quality of LLM-Generated Feedback
papers_to_add.finding: Key AWE corrective feedback study (227 citations on JSLW). Relevant to the category-level accuracy lineage.

papers_to_add.title: Navigating AI feedback in translation training: how text type, proficiency, and attitude shape students' acceptance behaviors
papers_to_add.finding: 68.2% acceptance rate for AI feedback; rejection triggers deeper cognitive processing. Supports the six-category uptake taxonomy distinguishing passive non-engagement from active rejection with self-revision.

papers_to_add.title: Beyond the monorail perspective: constructing a dual-role engagement framework in peer feedback for second language oral communication
papers_to_add.finding: Dual-role engagement framework: learners as both feedback providers and recipients. Complements Lin & Yu (2025) already cited.

papers_to_add.title: Peer feedback and Chinese medical students' English academic writing development: a longitudinal intervention study
papers_to_add.finding: Peer feedback effects retained at 6-week delayed post-test — supports the Study 3 delayed post-test design.

papers_to_add.title: Continuation Wing 1.0 - A Deepseek-Assisted Feedback Platform for Continuation Writing Assessment
papers_to_add.finding: DeepSeek platform for continuation writing with interpretable scoring. Demonstrates feasibility of multi-LLM feedback generation.

papers_to_add.title: LLM feedback for academic writing: Effects on students' performance and engagement
papers_to_add.finding: Examines LLM feedback effects on academic writing performance and engagement. Fleckenstein co-author connects to the meta-analysis already cited.
